node.Title: Announcing SCIM support for Cloudflare Access & Gateway
node.Description: Cloudflare Access & Gateway now support the System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) protocol.

node.Title: New: Scan Salesforce and Box for security issues
node.Description: Cloudflare CASB adds two new SaaS integrations for Salesforce and Box.

node.Title: API-based email scanning
node.Description: Customers can now onboard O365 domains to Area 1, adding more flexibility on deployment methods.

node.Title: Zone Versioning is now generally available
node.Description: Safely configure and deploy updates to zone configuration with Zone Versioning now Generally Available for Enterprise customers

node.Title: Expanding our Microsoft collaboration: proactive and automated Zero Trust security for customers
node.Description: As CIOs navigate the complexities of stitching together multiple solutions, we are extending our collaboration with Microsoft to create one of the best Zero Trust solutions available.

node.Title: Email Link Isolation: your safety net for the latest phishing attacks
node.Description: Email Link Isolation is your safety net for the odd links that end up in people’s inboxes, and they may click. This added protection turns Cloudflare Area 1 into the most comprehensive email security solution when it comes to protecting against phishing attacks.

node.Title: Improved access controls: API access can now be selectively disabled
node.Description: Making it easier for account owners to view and manage the access their users have on an account by allowing them to restrict API access to the account.

node.Title: One-click data security for your internal and SaaS applications
node.Description: Protect sensitive data on any Access app for any user on any device.

node.Title: How Cloudflare Area 1 and DLP work together to protect data in email
node.Description: Customers can leverage the power of Email Security and Cloudflare One to prevent data loss in their corporate network.

node.Title: How Cloudflare CASB and DLP work together to protect your data
node.Description: Cloudflare CASB and DLP will work together to protect sensitive data at rest.

node.Title: Announcing the Authorized Partner Service Delivery Track for Cloudflare One
node.Description: This week we’re announcing the Authorized Services Delivery Partner Track (ASDP) designed to validate partners that want to deliver services around Cloudflare One

node.Title: Cloudflare protection for all your cardinal directions
node.Description: In this post, we’ll recap how definitions of corporate network traffic have shifted and how Cloudflare One provides protection for all traffic flows, regardless of source or destination.

node.Title: Why do CIOs choose Cloudflare One?
node.Description: Today, over 10,000 organizations trust Cloudflare One to connect and secure their users, devices, applications, and data. As part of CIO Week, we spoke with the leaders of some of our largest customers to better understand why they selected Cloudflare

node.Title: New ways to troubleshoot Cloudflare Access 'blocked' messages
node.Description: Investigate, allow or block decisions based on how a connection was made with the same level of ease that you can troubleshoot user identity.

node.Title: Network detection and settings profiles for the Cloudflare One agent
node.Description: Cloudflare WARP can now securely detect pre-configured locations and route traffic based on the needs of the organization for that location.

node.Title: Announcing Custom DLP profiles
node.Description: Cloudflare Data Loss Prevention now offers the ability to create custom detections.

node.Title: Preview any Cloudflare product today
node.Description: Enterprise customers can now start previewing non-contracted services

node.Title: Announcing the Magic WAN Connector: the easiest on-ramp to your next generation network
node.Description: Cloudflare is making it even easier to get connected with the Magic WAN Connector: a lightweight software package you can install in any physical or cloud network to automatically connect, steer, and shape any IP traffic.

node.Title: Cloudflare DDoS threat report for 2022 Q4
node.Description: In Q4, Cloudflare mitigated millions of DDoS attacks. Attack durations increase, volumetric attacks surged, and ransom DDoS attacks persist. Travel & events industries were hit hardest and over 90% of traffic to Chinese Internet properties were L3/4 DDoS attacks. Read more on our recent report.

node.Title: Introducing Digital Experience Monitoring
node.Description: With Digital Experience Monitoring, we’ve set out to build the tools you need to quickly find the needle in the haystack and resolve issues related to performance and connectivity

node.Title: Cloudflare is faster than Zscaler
node.Description: Cloudflare is 38-55% faster at delivering Zero Trust experiences than Zscaler. Let’s talk about why that is and how we found that out

node.Title: Bring your own certificates to Cloudflare Gateway
node.Description: Security and IT administrators can now bring their own custom certificates to encrypt user side connections for Zero Trust

node.Title: Weave your own global, private, virtual Zero Trust network on Cloudflare with WARP-to-WARP
node.Description: Today, we’re excited to announce a new way to use Cloudflare WARP to securely connect to and from any device in your Zero Trust deployment simply running WARP

node.Title: Welcome to CIO Week 2023
node.Description: This CIO Week we’ll demonstrate how Cloudflare is helping CIOs keep data, devices and employees both safe and fast across hybrid and remote environments. We’ll show how Cloudflare accelerates digital transformation and modernizes networking and security towards a Zero Trust model

node.Title: How Cloudflare can help stop malware before it reaches your app
node.Description: Today, we’re making the job of application security teams easier, by providing a content scanning engine integrated with our Web Application Firewall (WAF), so that malicious files being uploaded by end users, never reach origin servers in the first place

node.Title: The state of HTTP in 2022
node.Description: So what happened at all of those working group meetings, specification documents, and side events in 2022? What are implementers and deployers of the web’s protocol doing? And what’s coming next?

node.Title: Cloudflare Radar 2022 Year in Review
node.Description: We’re excited to present the Cloudflare Radar 2022 Year In Review, featuring interactive charts, graphs, and maps you can use to explore what changed on the Internet throughout this past year

node.Title: One of our most requested features is here: DNS record comments and tags
node.Description: Today, we’re releasing DNS record comments and tags, making it easier to keep track of and manage DNS records of your domain

node.Title: Closing out 2022 with our latest Impact Report
node.Description: Our Impact Report is an annual summary highlighting how we are trying to build a better Internet and the progress we are making on our environmental, social, and governance priorities.

node.Title: Everything you might have missed during Cloudflare's Impact Week 2022
node.Description: And that’s a wrap! Impact Week 2022 has come to a close. Over the last week, Cloudflare announced new commitments in our mission to help build a better Internet, including delivering Zero Trust services for the most vulnerable voices and for critical infrastructure providers.

node.Title: The unintended consequences of blocking IP addresses
node.Description: A discussion about IP blocking: why we see it, what it is, what it does, who it affects, and why it’s such a problematic way to address content online.

node.Title: Introducing Cloudflare's Third Party Code of Conduct
node.Description: We are excited to share our Third Party Code of Conduct, specifically formulated with our suppliers, resellers and other partners in mind.

node.Title: Helping build a safer Internet by measuring BGP RPKI Route Origin Validation
node.Description: Is BGP safe yet? If the question needs asking, then it isn't. But how far the Internet is from this goal is what we set out to answer.

node.Title: The latest from Cloudflare's seventeen Employee Resource Groups
node.Description: The ERGs at Cloudflare have helped us realize the power of community and how critical it is for hybrid work.

node.Title: Working to help the HBCU Smart Cities Challenge
node.Description: Learn how Cloudflare is empowering the HBCU Smart Cities Challenge

node.Title: How Cloudflare advocates for a better Internet
node.Description: In this blog we outline how we advocate, across the many jurisdictions where we operate, for a better Internet, in our engagement with governments and regulators.

node.Title: A new, configurable and scalable version of Geo Key Manager, now available in Closed Beta
node.Description: We’re excited to announce a new version of Geo Key Manager — one that allows customers to define boundaries by country, by a region, or by a standard, such as “only store my private keys in FIPS compliant data centers” — now available in Closed Beta.

node.Title: How Cloudflare helps next-generation markets
node.Description: The speed of an Internet connection is more about decreasing real-world latency than adding underutilized bandwidth.

node.Title: Applying Human Rights Frameworks to our approach to abuse
node.Description: Cloudflare launched its first Human Rights Policy in 2021, formally stating our commitment to respect human rights under the UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights (UNGPs)

node.Title: Cloudflare is joining the AS112 project to help the Internet deal with misdirected DNS queries
node.Description: Cloudflare is participating in the AS112 project, becoming an operator of the loosely coordinated, distributed sink of the reverse lookup (PTR) queries for RFC 1918 addresses, dynamic DNS updates and other ambiguous addresses.

node.Title: Partnering with civil society to track Internet shutdowns with Radar Alerts and API
node.Description: Learn more on how Cloudflare works with civil society organizations to provide tools to track Internet shutdowns using Radar Alerts and API.

node.Title: Cloudflare achieves FedRAMP authorization to secure more of the public sector
node.Description: Cloudflare for Government expands Cloudflare’s ability to protect and secure the Public Sector

node.Title: More bots, more trees
node.Description: Cloudflare’s Bot Fight Mode caught 6x more bots in 2022, and we’re contributing to a new tree planting project in West Bengal.

node.Title: How we’re making Cloudflare’s infrastructure more sustainable
node.Description: Our hardware sustainability initiative encapsulates using hardware components for as long as possible, recycling them responsibly when it is time to decommission them, and selecting the most power-efficient options for our workloads.

node.Title: How we redesigned our offices to be more sustainable
node.Description: As we reimagine how our physical office spaces support the work we do at Cloudflare, sustainable design and operations is at the forefront of our design philosophy and one of the underlying premises that affects all aspects of our workplaces.

node.Title: Historical emissions offsets (and Scope 3 sneak preview)
node.Description: Today, we are excited to announce our first step toward offsetting our historic emissions by investing in 6,060 MTs worth of reforestation carbon offsets as part of the Pacajai Reduction of Emissions from Deforestation and forest Degradation (REDD+) Project in the State of Para, Brazil.

node.Title: A more sustainable end-of-life for your legacy hardware appliances with Cloudflare and Iron Mountain
node.Description: Introduce remarketing and recycling as the more sustainable ways of disposing of your legacy hardware appliances. And announce a partnership with Iron Mountain to make this journey easier and more cost beneficial for Cloudflare customers.

node.Title: Independent report shows: moving to Cloudflare can cut your carbon footprint
node.Description: Preliminary study results find Cloudflare product to be up to 90% more carbon efficient than equivalent on premise hardware.

node.Title: How Cloudflare helps protect small businesses
node.Description: In this blog, we’ll share new research on how small businesses, including those using our free services, have leveraged Cloudflare services to make their businesses more secure and resistant to disruption.

node.Title: Project Safekeeping – protecting the world’s most vulnerable infrastructure with Zero Trust
node.Description: Cloudflare’s mission is to help make a better Internet. Starting December 13, 2022, we will help support these vulnerable infrastructure by providing our enterprise-level Zero Trust cybersecurity solution to them at no cost, with no time limit.

node.Title: The US government is working on an “Internet for all” plan. We’re on board.
node.Description: The US government has a $65 billion program to get all Americans on the Internet. It’s a great initiative.

node.Title: Cloudflare expands Project Pangea to connect and protect (even) more community networks
node.Description: A year and a half ago, Cloudflare launched Project Pangea to help provide Internet services to underserved communities. Today, we're sharing what we've learned by partnering with community networks, and announcing an expansion of the project.

node.Title: The Montgomery, Alabama Internet Exchange is making the Internet faster. We’re happy to be there.
node.Description: Internet Exchanges are a critical part of a strong Internet. Here’s the story of one of them.

node.Title: Democratizing access to Zero Trust with Project Galileo
node.Description: Learn how organizations under Project Galileo use Cloudflare Zero Trust to protect their organization from cyberattacks.

node.Title: The challenges of sanctioning the Internet
node.Description: As governments continue to use sanctions as a foreign policy tool, we think it’s important that policymakers continue to hear from Internet infrastructure companies about how the legal framework is impacting their ability to support a global Internet.

node.Title: How Cloudflare helps secure the inboxes of democracy
node.Description: Cloudflare email security tool worked hard in the 2022 midterm elections to ensure that the email inboxes of those seeking office were secure.

node.Title: The latest on attacks, traffic patterns and cyber protection in Ukraine
node.Description: On February 24, 2022, when Russia invaded Ukraine, Cloudflare jumped into action to provide services that could help prevent potentially destructive cyber attacks and keep the global Internet flowing.

node.Title: Two months later: Internet use in Iran during the Mahsa Amini Protests
node.Description: A series of protests began in Iran on September 16, following the death in custody of Mahsa Amini — a 22-year-old who had been arrested for violating Iran’s mandatory hijab law. The protests and civil unrest have continued to this day.

node.Title: Expanding Area 1 email security to the Athenian Project
node.Description: We are excited to share that we have grown our offering under the Athenian Project to include Cloudflare’s Area 1 email security suite to help state and local governments protect against a broad spectrum of phishing attacks to keep voter data safe and secure.

node.Title: Cloudflare Zero Trust for Project Galileo and the Athenian Project
node.Description: Starting today, we are making the Cloudflare One Zero Trust suite available to teams that qualify for Projects Galileo or Athenian at no cost.

node.Title: Welcome to Cloudflare’s Impact Week
node.Description: Over the course of this Impact Week, we will tell other stories about the way that the Internet, and Cloudflare specifically, provide an optimistic opportunity to improve our world.

node.Title: New! Security Analytics provides a comprehensive view across all your traffic
node.Description: Security Analytics gives you a security lens across all of your HTTP traffic, not only mitigated requests, allowing you to focus on what matters most: traffic deemed malicious but potentially not mitigated.

node.Title: Stop attacks before they are known: making the Cloudflare WAF smarter
node.Description: Today we are making the WAF smarter by increasing availability of our new machine learning powered enhancement, the WAF Attack Score!

node.Title: Alex Kim: Why I joined Cloudflare
node.Description: I am excited to announce that as of November 1, I have joined Cloudflare as Country Manager of South Korea to help build a better Internet and to expand Cloudflare’s growing customer, partner, and local teams in Korea.

node.Title: Adjusting pricing, introducing annual plans, and accelerating innovation
node.Description: After not raising prices in our history, this was something we thought carefully about before deciding to do. While we have over a decade of network expansion and innovation under our belts, what may not be intuitive is that our goal is not to increase revenue from this change.

node.Title: Cloudflare partners to simplify China connectivity for corporate networks
node.Description: Today, along with our strategic partners, we’re excited to announce expansion of our Cloudflare One product suite to tackle these problems, with the goal of creating the best SASE experience for users and organizations in China

node.Title: Cloudflare partners to simplify China connectivity for corporate networks
node.Description: Today, along with our strategic partners, we’re excited to announce expansion of our Cloudflare One product suite to tackle these problems, with the goal of creating the best SASE experience for users and organizations in China

node.Title: The Linux Kernel Key Retention Service and why you should use it in your next application
node.Description: Many leaks happen because of software bugs and security vulnerabilities. In this post we will learn how the Linux kernel can help protect cryptographic keys from a whole class of potential security vulnerabilities: memory access violations.

node.Title: An early look at Thanksgiving 2022 Internet trends
node.Description: Yesterday, November 24, 2022, was Thanksgiving Day in the US. Last year, we saw how the US paused shopping (and browsing) for Thanksgiving. So, how was it this year?

node.Title: Cloudflare servers don't own IPs anymore – so how do they connect to the Internet?
node.Description:
In this blog we'll discuss how we manage Cloudflare IP addresses
used to retrieve the data from the Internet, how our egress
network design has evolved, how we optimized it for best use
of available IP space and introduce our soft-anycast technology

node.Title: Why BGP communities are better than AS-path prepends
node.Description: Routing on the Internet follows a few basic principles. Unfortunately not everything on the Internet is created equal, and prepending can do more harm than good. In this blog post we’ll talk about the problems that prepending aims to solve, and some alternative solutions

node.Title: How we detect route leaks and our new Cloudflare Radar route leak service
node.Description: In this blog post, we will introduce our new system designed to detect route leaks and its integration on Cloudflare Radar and its public API.

node.Title: Why Cloudflare’s one of the Top 100 Most Loved Workplaces in 2022
node.Description: We are proud to share that Cloudflare has been named one of the Top 100 Most Loved Workplaces in 2022 by Newsweek and Best Practice Institute (BPI)

node.Title: ICYMI: Developer Week 2022 announcements
node.Description: This week we made over 30 announcements, in case you missed any here’s a quick round-up.

node.Title: Send Cloudflare Workers logs to a destination of your choice with Workers Trace Events Logpush
node.Description: Workers Trace Events Logpush gives new levels of visibility into Workers invocations, console.log messages and errors. It is now available to everyone on the Workers Paid and Enterprise plans!

node.Title: Twilio Segment Edge SDK powered by Cloudflare Workers
node.Description: With Segment Edge SDK, built on Cloudflare Workers, developers can collect high-quality first-party data and use Segment Edge SDK to access real-time user profiles and state, to deliver personalized app experiences without managing a ton of infrastructure.

node.Title: Network performance update: Developer Week 2022
node.Description: Cloudflare did the measurements to prove we’re the fastest developer platform, beating out all the other competition.

node.Title: Improving Workers TypeScript support: accuracy, ergonomics and interoperability
node.Description: Today, we’re excited to announce the next major release of Workers TypeScript definitions with a bunch of improvements, and the open-sourcing of the new automatic generation scripts.

node.Title: Doubling down on local development with Workers: Miniflare meets workerd
node.Description: Today, we’re taking local development to the next level by releasing Miniflare 3, powered by the open-source workerd runtime, along with support for migrating existing dashboard projects and using real data locally.

node.Title: How Cloudflare instruments services using Workers Analytics Engine
node.Description: Learn how Cloudflare uses our own Workers Analytics Engine product to capture analytics about our own products!

node.Title: Spice up your sites on Cloudflare Pages with Pages Functions General Availability
node.Description: Pages is officially a full stack platform with Pages Functions now Generally Available. Pages is harnessing the power and scalability of Workers and specializing them to align with the Pages developer experience.

node.Title: Announcing the first Workers Launchpad cohort and growth of the program to $2 billion
node.Description: Announcing the Fall 2022 cohort of 25 Workers Launchpad startups and growth of the program from $1.25BN to $2BN

node.Title: How we built it: the technology behind Cloudflare Radar 2.0
node.Description: Radar 2.0 was launched last month during Cloudflare's Birthday Week as a complete product revamp. This blog explains how we built it technically. Hopefully, it will inspire other developers to build complex web apps using Cloudflare products.

node.Title: Server-side render full stack applications with Pages Functions
node.Description: Page Functions are now out of beta, bringing the ability to add dynamic server-side rendering to your applications. Pages makes it easy to deploy applications built using all the major frameworks.

node.Title: How Cloudflare uses Terraform to manage Cloudflare
node.Description: Cloudflare uses the Cloudflare Terraform provider extensively to make changes to our internal accounts as easy as opening a pull request.

node.Title: The most programmable Supercloud with Cloudflare Snippets
node.Description: Today we are announcing Cloudflare Snippets. Snippets are a simple way of executing a small piece of Javascript on select HTTP requests, using the ruleset engine filtering logic.

node.Title: Keep track of Workers’ code and configuration changes with Deployments
node.Description: Today we’re happy to announce Deployments for Workers. Deployments allow developers to audit changes made to their applications.

node.Title: Incremental adoption of micro-frontends with Cloudflare Workers
node.Description: With Cloudflare Workers, our fragment-based micro-frontend architecture, and fragment piercing technique, engineering teams can incrementally improve large frontends in a fraction of the time, yielding significant user and developer experience gains.

node.Title: UPDATE Supercloud SET status = 'open alpha' WHERE product = 'D1';
node.Description: As we continue down the road to making D1 production ready, it wouldn’t be “the Cloudflare way” unless we stopped for feedback first. D1 is now in Open Alpha!

node.Title: Bringing authentication and identification to Workers through Mutual TLS
node.Description: We’re excited to announce that Workers will soon be able to send outbound requests through a mutually authenticated channel via mutual TLS authentication!

node.Title: The Cloudflare API now uses OpenAPI schemas
node.Description: Cloudflare now has OpenAPI Schemas available for the API. Users can use these schemas in any open source OpenAPI Tooling.

node.Title: Iteration isn't just for code: here are our latest API docs
node.Description: We’re excited to share that the next iteration of Cloudflare’s API reference documentation is now available. The new docs standardize our API content and improve the overall developer experience for interacting with Cloudflare’s API.

node.Title: Making static sites dynamic with Cloudflare D1
node.Description: In this blog post, I'll show you how to use D1 to add comments to a static blog site. To do this, we'll construct a new D1 database and build a simple JSON API that allows the creation and retrieval of comments.

node.Title: Automate an isolated browser instance with just a few lines of code
node.Description: Workers Browser Rendering API is our out-of-the-box solution for simplifying developer workflows, including capturing images or screenshots, by running browser automation in Workers. If you’re interested, sign up for the waitlist.

node.Title: Xata Workers: client-side database access without client-side secrets
node.Description: Xata is on a mission to solve the industry’s hardest data problems with their Serverless Data Platform. We’re excited to have Xata building their serverless functions product – Xata Workers – on top of Workers for Platforms. Here is Xata’s story.

node.Title: Migrate from S3 easily with the R2 Super Slurper
node.Description: Today we're announcing the R2 Super Slurper, the tool that will enable you to migrate all your data to R2 in a simple and efficient way

node.Title: Reduce origin load, save on cloud egress fees, and maximize cache hits with Cache Reserve
node.Description: Today we’re extremely excited to announce that Cache Reserve is graduating to open beta – users will now be able to test it and integrate it into their content delivery strategy without any additional waiting

node.Title: Indexing millions of HTTP requests using Durable Objects
node.Description: A deep-dive into how Cloudflare uses Durable Objects and the Streams API to index millions of HTTP requests stored in R2

node.Title: Store and process your Cloudflare Logs... with Cloudflare
node.Description: Today we’re announcing Cloudflare Logs Engine — a new system that will enable you to do anything you need with Cloudflare Logs, all within Cloudflare

node.Title: Get started with Cloudflare Workers with ready-made templates
node.Description: Today, we’re excited to share a collection of ready-made templates to help you build your next application on Cloudflare Workers

node.Title: Easy Postgres integration on Cloudflare Workers with Neon.tech
node.Description: Neon.tech is a database platform that allows you to branch your (Postgres compatible) database exactly like your code repository. And with the release of their Cloudflare Workers integration you can get started in minutes

node.Title: Welcome to the Supercloud (and Developer Week 2022)
node.Description: Every developer wants to get code running on one machine and perfect it. It’s so much easier to work that way. We just happen to have one machine that scales to the size of the Internet: a global, distributed, supercomputer. It’s our Supercloud, and we build our own products on it, and you can too

node.Title: Cloudflare Workers scale too well and broke our infrastructure, so we are rebuilding it on Workers
node.Description: DevCycle re-architected their feature management tool using Workers for improved scalability.

node.Title: Build applications of any size on Cloudflare with the Queues open beta
node.Description: Cloudflare Queues enables developers to build performant and resilient distributed applications that span the globe. Any developer with a paid Workers plan can enroll in the Open Beta and start building today!

node.Title: The road to a more standards-compliant Workers API
node.Description: We have been making improvements in the Workers runtime to increase alignment with Web Platform standards around event handling, task cancellation using AbortController, text encoding and decoding, URL parsing and pattern matching, and streams support.

node.Title: 2022 US midterm elections attack analysis
node.Description: For Athenian Project and Cloudflare for Campaigns participant websites, overall traffic volume ramped as Election Day approached

node.Title: Protecting election groups during the 2022 US midterm elections
node.Description: We at Cloudflare are proud to be able to play a role in helping safeguard the integrity of the electoral process. Through our Impact programs, we provide cyber security products to help protect access to authoritative voting information and the security of sensitive voter data

node.Title: How the Brazilian Presidential elections affected Internet traffic
node.Description: What happens to the Internet traffic of a country when an important election happens. In Brazil, the first round of the Presidential elections brought a 10% decrease in Internet traffic, but the runoff on Sunday had a bigger: 21% drop

node.Title: Cloudflare is not affected by the OpenSSL vulnerabilities CVE-2022-3602 and CVE-2022-37
node.Description: Information on CVE-2022-3602 and CVE-2022-3786, and why Cloudflare was not impacted

node.Title: Privacy Gateway: a privacy preserving proxy built on Internet standards
node.Description: Privacy Gateway enables privacy-forward applications to use Cloudflare as a trusted Relay, limiting which identifying information, including IP addresses, is visible to their infrastructure

node.Title: Stronger than a promise: proving Oblivious HTTP privacy properties
node.Description: In this blog post, we describe a formal, computer-aided security analysis of Oblivious HTTP, an emerging IETF standard that applications can use to improve user privacy

node.Title: Partial Cloudflare outage on October 25, 2022
node.Description: Today, a change to our Tiered Cache system caused some requests to fail for users with status code 530

node.Title: Email Routing leaves Beta
node.Description: Today Email Routing leaves Beta and an update on all the new things we've been adding to the service, including behind-the-scenes and not-so-visible improvements

node.Title: And here's another one: the Next.js Edge Runtime becomes the fourth full-stack framework supported by Cloudflare Pages
node.Description: You can now deploy SSR Next.js applications to the Cloudflare Pages platform ⚡️

node.Title: Page Shield can now watch for malicious outbound connections made by third-party JavaScript code
node.Description: Starting today, Page Shield can now watch for malicious outbound connections made by third-party JavaScript code

node.Title: Cloudflare Workers and micro-frontends: made for one another
node.Description: In this blog-post we demonstrate how hosting and combining multiple server-side rendered micro-frontends on Cloudflare Workers offer a highly scalable, high performance solution to these problems

node.Title: Making peering easy with the new Cloudflare Peering Portal
node.Description: Cloudflare's peering portal allows users to log in with existing PeeringDB credentials and request peering sessions with cloudflare direct from the portal

node.Title: Internet disruptions overview for Q3 2022
node.Description: In this post, we review selected Internet disruptions observed by Cloudflare during the second quarter of 2022, supported by traffic graphs from Cloudflare Radar and other internal Cloudflare tools, and grouped by associated cause or common geography

node.Title: Cloudflare DDoS threat report 2022 Q3
node.Description: In Q3, DDoS attacks increased by 111% YoY, Cloudflare auto-mitigated a 2.5 Tbps attack targeting a Minecraft server as multi-terabit scale DDoS attacks become increasingly frequent. Read more in our 2022 Q3 DDoS Report

node.Title: Assembly within! BPF tail calls on x86 and ARM
node.Description: We have first adopted the BPF tail calls when building our XDP-based packet processing pipeline. BPF tail calls have served us well since then. But they do have their caveats

node.Title: Cloudflare Pages gets even faster with Early Hints
node.Description: Announcing support for sending Early Hints from your Cloudflare Pages projects

node.Title: Total TLS: one-click TLS for every hostname you have
node.Description: Today, we’re excited to announce Total TLS — a one-click feature that will issue individual TLS certificates for every subdomain in our customer’s domains

node.Title: Join our upcoming live roadshow series: ‘Zero Trust, Zero Nonsense’
node.Description: Starting in a couple of weeks, and continuing through the end of the year, we’re hosting a series of Zero Trust Roadshows in various North American cities

node.Title: Bringing the best live video experience to Cloudflare Stream with AV1
node.Description: Cloudflare Stream now supports the AV1 codec for live video in open beta, unlocking live-streaming at higher resolution, with lower bandwidth

node.Title: What we served up for the last Birthday Week before we're a teenager
node.Description: Cloudflare's 12th birthday week: 36 announcements ranging from SIM cards to post quantum encryption via hardware keys and so much more. Here’s a review of everything we announced this week

node.Title: Defending against future threats: Cloudflare goes post-quantum
node.Description: The future of a private and secure Internet is at stake; that is why today we have enabled post-quantum cryptography support for all our customers

node.Title: Automatic (secure) transmission: taking the pain out of origin connection security
node.Description: Today we’re excited to announce that we will soon be offering a zero-configuration option for security on Cloudflare. If we find that we can automatically upgrade the security connection between Cloudflare and a user’s origin, we will

node.Title: Introducing post-quantum Cloudflare Tunnel
node.Description: Every connection we make post-quantum secure, we remove one opportunity for compromise: that's why we are announcing post-quantum Cloudflare Tunnel to help you secure every connection to our network

node.Title: Don't roll your own high cardinality analytics, use Workers Analytics Engine
node.Description: Join our open beta and start collecting telemetry about anything using Cloudflare Workers today!

node.Title: Project A11Y: how we upgraded Cloudflare’s dashboard to adhere to industry accessibility standards
node.Description: At Cloudflare, we believe the Internet should be accessible to everyone. And today, we’re happy to announce a more inclusive Cloudflare dashboard experience: adherence to the industry accessibility standards, including WCAG 2.1 AA and section 508 compliance.

node.Title: Lights, Camera, Action! Business and Pro customers get bundled streaming video
node.Description: If you have a Business or Pro subscription, soon you will receive a free allocation of Cloudflare Stream.

node.Title: The home page for Internet insights: Cloudflare Radar 2.0
node.Description: Cloudflare Radar makes Internet trends, patterns and insights available to everyone, and we’re now making them even easier to find, understand and share.

node.Title: The status page the Internet needs: Cloudflare Radar Outage Center
node.Description: The new Cloudflare Radar Outage Center (CROC), launched as part of Radar 2.0, is intended to be an archive of information about Internet outages observed by Cloudflare

node.Title: Goodbye, Alexa. Hello, Cloudflare Radar Domain Rankings
node.Description: Today, we are launching a new dataset called Radar Rankings, where we identify the top most popular domains that reflect how people use the Internet globally and per country.

node.Title: Gateway + CASB: alphabetti spaghetti that spells better SaaS security
node.Description: Now that Cloudflare CASB is Generally Available, let’s take a look at how users have been leveraging other Cloudflare Zero Trust products with CASB, starting with Gateway

node.Title: Click Here! (safely): Automagical Browser Isolation for potentially unsafe links in email
node.Description: There’s always a cat and mouse game between hackers and security companies. New attacks try to weaponize website links after emails have been delivered to mailboxes, and Email Link Isolation is here to revolutionize protection against those attacks.

node.Title: Back in 2017 we gave you Unmetered DDoS Mitigation, here's a birthday gift: Unmetered Rate Limiting
node.Description: Starting today, Free, Pro and Business plans include Rate Limiting rules without additional charges.

node.Title: Now all customers can share access to their Cloudflare account with Role Based Access Controls
node.Description: Role Based Access Controls are now available for every plan!

node.Title: How Cloudflare implemented hardware keys with FIDO2 and Zero Trust to prevent phishing
node.Description: Adopting a phishing resistant second factor, like a YubiKey with FIDO2, is the number one way to prevent phishing attacks. Cloudflare has used phishing resistant second factors only since February 2021, and these were the steps we took to accomplish that.

node.Title: The (hardware) key to making phishing defense seamless with Cloudflare Zero Trust and Yubico
node.Description: Announcing a new collaboration with Yubico, to remove any barriers for organizations of any size to deploying hardware security keys.

node.Title: Announcing Turnstile, a user-friendly, privacy-preserving alternative to CAPTCHA
node.Description: Any website can use a simple API to replace CAPTCHAs with our invisible alternative, whether they’re on the Cloudflare network or not.

node.Title: We've shipped so many products the Cloudflare dashboard needed its own search engine
node.Description: Today we’re proud to announce our beta release of quick search for the Cloudflare dashboard, our first ever cross-dashboard search tool to help you navigate our products and features.

node.Title: Private by design: building privacy-preserving products with Cloudflare's Privacy Edge
node.Description: Introducing Privacy Edge – a collection of products that make it easier for site owners and developers to protect their users’ privacy by default.

node.Title: Introducing Cloudflare’s free Botnet Threat Feed for service providers
node.Description: We’re pleased to introduce Cloudflare’s free Botnet Threat Feed for Service Providers. This includes all types of service providers, ranging from hosting providers to ISPs and cloud compute providers.

node.Title: Monitor your own network with free network flow analytics from Cloudflare
node.Description: Cloudflare is excited to announce that we are releasing a free version of Magic Networking Monitoring (previously called Flow Based Monitoring). Magic Network Monitoring receives network flow data from a customer’s router(s) and provides network traffic analytics via Cloudflare’s dashboard.

node.Title: Cloudflare named a Leader in WAF by Forrester
node.Description: Forester has recognised Cloudflare as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™: Web Application Firewalls, Q3 2022 report. The report evaluated 12 Web Application Firewall (WAF) providers on 24 criteria across current offering, strategy and market presence.

node.Title: Build your next startup on Cloudflare with our comprehensive Startup Plan, v2.0
node.Description: The Startup Plan now has an expanded product basket and broader eligibility criteria, which means more startups now qualify for the program.

node.Title: The world’s leading venture capitalist firms commit $1.25 BILLION to back startups built on Cloudflare Workers
node.Description: Building and scaling a startup is hard work. Apply to the $1.5BN the Workers Launchpad funding program for an opportunity to pitch leading VCs and other perks

node.Title: D1: our quest to simplify databases
node.Description: Get an inside look on the D1 experience today, what the team is currently working on and what’s coming up!

node.Title: Introducing Cache Rules: precision caching at your fingertips
node.Description: We have spent the last ten years learning how customers use Page Rules to customize their cached content, and it’s clear the time is ripe for evolving rules-based caching on Cloudflare

node.Title: WebRTC live streaming to unlimited viewers, with sub-second latency
node.Description: Cloudflare Stream now supports live streaming over WebRTC to unlimited concurrent viewers, using open standards WHIP and WHEP, with zero dependencies or client SDKs necessary.

node.Title: Introducing workerd: the Open Source Workers runtime
node.Description: workerd is the JavaScript/Wasm runtime code that powers Cloudflare Workers, now open source under the Apache 2.0 license

node.Title: Build real-time video and audio apps on the world’s most interconnected network
node.Description: We are announcing Cloudflare Calls, a new product that lets developers build real-time audio and video apps

node.Title: Dynamic URL redirects: 301 to the future
node.Description: With Dynamic redirects, users can redirect visitors to another webpage or website based upon hundreds of options such as the visitor's country of origin or language, without having to write a single line of code

node.Title: Where to? Introducing Origin Rules
node.Description: Origin Rules is a dedicated product for ‘where does this traffic go where it leaves Cloudflare.’ Customers are able to match on an HTTP request using filters and override the host, port, SNI, and the origin a request resolves to.

node.Title: Introducing Configuration Rules
node.Description: Configuration Rules is the new and improved way for Cloudflare customers to control and manipulate features that are enabled or disabled for specific requests on their zone.

node.Title: The future of Page Rules
node.Description: Learn about four new products that will eventually replace Page Rules by putting more power into the hands of users.

node.Title: Cloudflare Queues: globally distributed queues without the egress fees
node.Description: Cloudflare Queues is a message queuing service that allows applications to reliably send and receive messages using Cloudflare Workers

node.Title: Bringing Zero Trust to mobile network operators
node.Description: Better together: 5G mobile networks and Cloudflare’s all-in-one SASE platform

node.Title: Securing the Internet of Things
node.Description: We’ve been defending customers from Internet of Things botnets for years now, and it’s time to turn the tides: we’re bringing the same security behind our Zero Trust platform to IoT

node.Title: The first Zero Trust SIM
node.Description: We’re announcing the first Zero Trust SIM: the next major part of Cloudflare One, combining both software and hardware layers to rethink mobile device security for organizations

node.Title: Cloudflare’s 2022 Annual Founders’ Letter
node.Description: Cloudflare launched on September 27, 2010. This week we'll celebrate our 12th birthday. As has become our tradition, we'll be announcing a series of products that we think of as our gifts back to the Internet

node.Title: GA Week 2022: what you may have missed
node.Description: We made a lot of new products Generally Available this week, so in case you missed it, here’s a recap

node.Title: How to enable Private Access Tokens in iOS 16 and stop seeing CAPTCHAs
node.Description: Nobody likes CAPTCHAs. Now, with iOS 16 on iPhones, a small enabled toggle can make them disappear while improving privacy on your device. Here’s how to check if it’s “on” and try it out

node.Title: Logpush: now lower cost and with more visibility
node.Description: Logpush jobs can now be filtered to contain only logs of interest. Also, you can receive alerts when jobs are failing, as well as get statistics on the health of your jobs

node.Title: Cloudflare Zaraz supports Managed Components and DLP to make third-party tools private
node.Description: Third party tools are the only thing you can’t control on your website, unless you use Managed Components with Cloudflare Zaraz

node.Title: API Endpoint Management and Metrics are now GA
node.Description: API Shield customers can save, update, and monitor the performance of API endpoints

node.Title: Protests spur Internet disruptions in Iran
node.Description: The Iranian government has disrupted Internet connectivity in response to recent protests over the death of a woman while in police custody

node.Title: Regional Services comes to India, Japan and Australia
node.Description: With Regional Services, we are thrilled to expand our coverage to these countries in Asia Pacific, allowing more customers to use Cloudflare by giving them precise control over which parts of the Cloudflare network are able to perform advanced functions

node.Title: Regional Services llega a India, Japón y Australia
node.Description: Estamos encantados de ampliar nuestro alcance a estos países de Asia-Pacífico, permitiendo así que más clientes utilicen Cloudflare, dándoles un control preciso sobre qué partes de la red de Cloudflare pueden realizar funciones avanzadas

node.Title: Store and retrieve your logs on R2
node.Description: Log Storage on R2: a cost-effective solution to store event logs for any of our products

node.Title: SVG support in Cloudflare Images
node.Description: Cloudflare Images now supports storing and delivering SVG files

node.Title: Going originless with Cloudflare Workers – Building a Todo app – Part 1: The API
node.Description: Custom Domains are now in Generally Available! Today we go through Part 1 in a series on building completely serverless applications on Cloudflare’s Developer Platform

node.Title: The easiest way to build a modern SaaS application
node.Description: With Workers for Platforms, your customers can build custom logic to meet their needs right into your application. We’re excited to announce that Workers for Platforms is now in GA for all Enterprise customers

node.Title: Stream Live is now Generally Available
node.Description: Stream Live is now out of beta, available to everyone, and ready for production traffic at scale

node.Title: R2 is now Generally Available
node.Description: R2 is now generally available!! R2 gives developers object storage minus the egress fees

node.Title: Cloudflare Area 1 - how the best email security keeps getting better
node.Description: Cloudflare started using Area 1 in 2020 and proceeded with acquiring the company in 2022. We were most impressed how phishing, responsible for 90+% of cyberattacks, basically became a non-issue overnight when we deployed Area 1. But our vision is much bigger than preventing phishing attacks

node.Title: Isolate browser-borne threats on any network with WAN-as-a-Service
node.Description: Defend any network from browser-borne threats with Cloudflare Browser Isolation by connecting legacy firewalls over IPSec / GRE

node.Title: Detect security issues in your SaaS apps with Cloudflare CASB
node.Description: Connect and scan your third-party SaaS apps for file leaks, misconfigurations, and Shadow IT, all in just a few clicks. Cloudflare CASB now Generally Available

node.Title: Cloudflare One Partner Program acceleration
node.Description: Cloudflare One Partner Program gains traction with existing and prospective partners

node.Title: Cloudflare Data Loss Prevention now Generally Available
node.Description: Data Loss Prevention is now available for Cloudflare contract customers, giving customers more options to protect their sensitive data

node.Title: Introducing Advanced DDoS Alerts
node.Description: Cloudflare’s Advanced DDoS Alerts provide tailored and actionable notifications in real-time

node.Title: Introducing Cloudflare Adaptive DDoS Protection - our new traffic profiling system for mitigating DDoS attacks
node.Description: Cloudflare’s new Adaptive DDoS Protection system learns your unique traffic patterns and constantly adapts to protect you against sophisticated DDoS attacks

node.Title: Account WAF now available to Enterprise customers
node.Description: Do you manage more than a single domain? If the answer is yes, now you can manage a single WAF configuration for all your enterprise domains

node.Title: Improved Access Control: Domain Scoped Roles are now generally available
node.Description: Starting today, it is possible to scope your users’ access to specific domains with Domain Scoped Roles becoming generally available

node.Title: Cloudforce One is now generally available: empower your security team with threat data, tooling, and access to industry experts
node.Description: Cloudflare’s threat operations and research team, Cloudforce One, is now open for business and has begun conducting threat briefings. Join our webinar on “YackingYeti: How a Russian threat group targets Ukraine—and the world”, scheduled for October 12, to learn more

node.Title: Welcome to GA Week
node.Description: Over the course of the next week, you’re going to hear about Cloudflare products that are out of beta and generally available

node.Title: Using Cloudflare R2 as an apt/yum repository
node.Description: Host your apt/yum repositories like how Cloudflare Tunnel does. Here's how

node.Title: How we built Pingora, the proxy that connects Cloudflare to the Internet
node.Description: Today we are excited to talk about Pingora, a new HTTP proxy we’ve built in-house using Rust that serves over 1 trillion requests a day

node.Title: Ethereum Gateway support for Görli + Sepolia Testnets and the Ethereum Merge
node.Description: Today we are excited to announce support for the upcoming Ethereum Merge and Sepolia and Görli testnet support

node.Title: Cloudflare named a Leader by Gartner
node.Description: Gartner has recognised Cloudflare as a Leader in the 2022 "Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Web Application and API Protection (WAAP)" report that evaluated 11 vendors for their ‘ability to execute’ and ‘completeness of vision’

node.Title: Improving the accuracy of our machine learning WAF using data augmentation and sampling
node.Description: Data Generation and Augmentation methods to train an effective machine learning WAF

node.Title: Blocking Kiwifarms
node.Description: We have blocked Kiwifarms. Visitors to any of the Kiwifarms sites that use any of Cloudflare's services will see a Cloudflare block page and a link to this post.

node.Title: (Untitled)
Log analytics using ClickHouse
node.Description: When a request at Cloudflare throws an error, information gets logged in our requests_error pipeline. The error logs are used to help troubleshoot customer-specific or network-wide issues

node.Title: Cloudflare's abuse policies & approach
node.Description: Cloudflare launched nearly twelve years ago. Over that time, our set of services has become much more complicated. With that complexity we have developed policies around how we handle abuse of different features Cloudflare provides

node.Title: Cloudflare 的滥用处理政策和方法
node.Description: Cloudflare 创立于近 12 年前。自创立以来，我们的服务集复杂程度已大幅提高。鉴于这种复杂性，针对不同 Cloudflare 功能，我们制定了处理滥用的政策。

node.Title: Políticas y enfoque de Cloudflare contra el abuso
node.Description: Cloudflare comenzó su andadura hace casi doce años. Desde nuestros inicios, nuestro conjunto de servicios han ganado en complejidad. Con ello en mente, hemos desarrollado políticas sobre cómo hacemos frente al abuso de las diferentes funciones de Cloudflare.

node.Title: Cloudflareの不正利用に対するポリシーとその取り組み
node.Description: Cloudflareは12年前に創立されました。サービスの立ち上げ以来、時間の経過とともに当社のサービスははるかに複雑なものになりました。このような複雑さの中で、私たちはCloudflareのさまざまな機能が不正使用された場合の扱いについてポリシーを策定しました。

node.Title: Cloudflare: Richtlinien zu & Umgang mit Regelverstößen
node.Description: Cloudflare wurde vor fast zwölf Jahren gegründet. Seit unserer Gründung ist unser Serviceangebot im Laufe der Zeit immer komplexer geworden. Mit dieser Komplexität haben wir Richtlinien entwickelt, wie wir mit Regelverstößen bzw. dem Missbrauch der verschiedenen Cloudflare-Funktionen umgehen.

node.Title: Politiques et approche de Cloudflare en matière d'utilisation abusive
node.Description: Cloudflare a été fondée voilà près de douze ans. Notre portefeuille de services est devenu beaucoup plus complexe. Avec cette complexité, nous avons élaboré des politiques déterminant de quelle façon nous traitons l'utilisation abusive des différentes fonctionnalités de Cloudflare.

node.Title: Introducing thresholds in Security Event Alerting: a z-score love story
node.Description: Today we are excited to announce thresholds for our Security Event Alerts: a new and improved way of detecting anomalous spikes of security events on your Internet properties. By introducing a threshold, we are able to make alerts more accurate and only notify you when it truly matters

node.Title: Performance isolation in a multi-tenant database environment
node.Description: Enforcing stricter performance isolation across neighboring tenants who rely on our storage infrastructure

node.Title: Open sourcing our fork of PgBouncer
node.Description: We are releasing our internal fork of Postgres, filled with authentication bug fixes and new features around per user and connection pool isolation

node.Title: Deep dives & how the Internet works
node.Description: We have amazing deep dives in our blog, but also research and how the Internet works kind of stories. Here are some highlights from 2022, and before (with glimpses of our history).

node.Title: Cloudflare Support Portal gets an overhaul
node.Description: The Cloudflare Support team has launched a new Support Portal. The portal will give you access to self-help resources, diagnostics with troubleshooting guides, and will provide for easier ticket submission

node.Title: Crawler Hints supports Microsoft’s IndexNow in helping users find new content
node.Description: Cloudflare is uniquely positioned to help give crawlers hints about when they should recrawl, if new content has been added, or if content on a site has recently changed

node.Title: 2022 attacks! An August reading list to go “Shields Up”
node.Description: In 2022, cybersecurity, more than ever, is a must-have for those who don’t want to take chances on getting caught in a cyberattack with difficult to deal with consequences. Here’s a reading list what you need to know about attacks that is also a guide on how to be protected

node.Title: The mechanics of a sophisticated phishing scam and how we stopped it
node.Description: Yesterday, August 8, 2022, Twilio shared that they’d been compromised by a targeted phishing attack. Around the same time as Twilio was attacked, we saw an attack with very similar characteristics also targeting Cloudflare’s employees

node.Title: Introducing new Cloudflare for SaaS documentation
node.Description: Cloudflare for SaaS offers a suite of Cloudflare products and add-ons to improve the security, performance, and reliability of SaaS providers. Now, the Cloudflare for SaaS documentation outlines how to optimize it in order to meet your goals

node.Title: 1.1.1.1 + WARP: More features, still private
node.Description: We’re announcing two major improvements to our 1.1.1.1 + WARP apps

node.Title: Build your next big idea with Cloudflare Pages
node.Description: Pages now supports direct uploads to give you more power to build and iterate how you want and with the tools you want.

node.Title: Building scheduling system with Workers and Durable Objects
node.Description: In this post we're going to show you how to build a scalable service that will schedule HTTP requests on a specific schedule or as one-off at a specific time

node.Title: Experiment with post-quantum cryptography today
node.Description: The future is post quantum. Enable post-quantum key agreement on your test zone today and get a headstart

node.Title: Building and using Managed Components with WebCM
node.Description: This is how Managed Components can be useful for you right now, if you manage a website or if you’re building third-party tools

node.Title: Load Balancing with Weighted Pools
node.Description: Today, we’re excited to announce a frequently requested feature for our Load Balancer – Weighted Pools

node.Title: Running Zig with WASI on Cloudflare Workers
node.Description: After the recent announcement regarding WASI support in Workers I decided to see what it would take to get code written in Zig to run as a Worker, and it turned out to be pretty trivial. This post documents the process I followed

node.Title: When the window is not fully open, your TCP stack is doing more than you think
node.Description: In this blog post I'll share my journey deep into the Linux networking stack, trying to understand the memory and window management of the receiving side of a TCP connection

node.Title: Cloudflare deployment in Guam
node.Description: Cloudflare Deployment in Guam - Delivering a Better Internet for Faraway Pacific Ocean Archipelagos Residents

node.Title: Using Apache Kafka to process 1 trillion inter-service messages
node.Description: We learnt a lot about Kafka on the way to 1 trillion messages, and built some interesting internal tools to ease adoption that will be explored in this blog post

node.Title: A story about AF_XDP, network namespaces and a cookie
node.Description: A crash in a development version of flowtrackd (the daemon that powers our Advanced TCP Protection) highlighted the fact that libxdp (and specifically the AF_XDP part) was not Linux network namespace aware. This blogpost describes the debugging journey to find the bug, as well as a fix.

node.Title: Working in public — our docs-as-code approach
node.Description: Besides following a docs-as-code approach, at Cloudflare we handle our documentation changes in a public repository. The contributions we get from the entire Cloudflare community help us make our documentation better every day

node.Title: How the James Webb Telescope's cosmic pictures impacted the Internet
node.Description: We show how the July 11-12 James Webb Telescope images that were revealed had an impact on DNS traffic in NASA and ESA websites, video and image hosting platforms, and news websites. The ‘cosmic’ results may be surprising

node.Title: Mantis - the most powerful botnet to date
node.Description: We named the botnet that launched the 26M rps (requests per second) DDoS attack "Mantis" as it is also like the Mantis shrimp, small but very powerful

node.Title: 35,000 new trees in Nova Scotia
node.Description: Cloudflare is proud to announce the first 35,000 trees from our commitment to help clean up bad bots (and the climate) have been planted

node.Title: Waiting Room Event Scheduling protects your site during online events
node.Description: With Event Scheduling for Waiting Room, you can protect your servers from being overloaded during your event while delivering a user experience that is unique to the occasion and consistent with your brand

node.Title: Introducing Location-Aware DDoS Protection
node.Description: Location-Aware DDoS Protection is now available in beta for Cloudflare Enterprise customers that are subscribed to the Advanced DDoS service

node.Title: NIST’s pleasant post-quantum surprise
node.Description: On Tuesday, the US National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) announced which post-quantum cryptography they will standardize. We were already drafting this post with an educated guess on the choice NIST would make.

node.Title: Cloudflare’s view of the Rogers Communications outage in Canada
node.Description: An outage at one of the largest ISPs in Canada, Rogers Communications, started earlier today, July 8, 2022, and is ongoing (eight hours and counting), and is impacting businesses and consumers.

node.Title: Automatic Signed Exchanges may dramatically boost your site visitor numbers
node.Description: Automatic Signed Exchanges may dramatically boost your site visitor numbers

node.Title: Announcing support for WASI on Cloudflare Workers
node.Description: Today, we are announcing experimental support for WASI (the WebAssembly System Interface) on Cloudflare Workers and support within wrangler2 to make it a joy to work with

node.Title: New WAF intelligence feeds
node.Description: Cloudflare is expanding our WAF’s threat intelligence capabilities by adding four new managed IP lists that can be used as part of any custom firewall rule
